title: TriadicGPT: End-to-End Prime Factorization in a Generative Language Model — Learned Algebraic Encoding from Joint Training
description: A 40M-parameter GPT language model augmented with a triadic projection head that produces discrete prime-factor signatures alongside standard next-token predictions. Learns triadic representations end-to-end through a dual-objective training loss combining language modeling with embedding alignment. Across 29+ training runs we demonstrate five principal findings: negligible language cost (+1.7% PPL), emergent semantic ordering at ~20M params, GPT-2 transfer closing 48% of gap to Engine PCA, iFSQ activation resolving the subsumption-language tradeoff (87.1% held-out at k=64), and compositional structure with sub-linear error accumulation (98.1% round-trip > 81.9% multiplicative). External SimLex-999 evaluation positions the system as algebraic encoding without semantic grounding; sparse fine-tuning reveals a sparsity-compositionality trade-off. Includes triadic-head (standalone PyPI package), desktop UI (PySide6), reptimeline (training-dynamics tool with MNIST/Pythia-70M backend validation), and full training/evaluation pipeline.
